AREC380 Data Science for Environmental and Resource Economics (3 Credits)

An introduction to principles of data science using modern, open source software tools with applications to important problems in environmental, energy and resource economics. Topics include data wrangling, exploratory data analysis and visualization, modeling, forecasting, practices for reproducible research, and communication of results. Prerequisite: AREC240, AREC241, AREC250, or ECON200.
